data.title: SKILLS VS. CLAUDE.MD: THE BOUNDARY
blocks: Both are markdown files Claude reads. The difference is when Claude reads them.
Skills vs. CLAUDE.md: the boundary — CLAUDE.md loads at session start. Every prompt in the session operates with CLAUDE.md in context. Use CLAUDE.md for what

data.title: THE TWO INVOCATION MODES.
blocks: Skills can be invoked two ways. The flag in the frontmatter controls which.
The two invocation modes — disable-model-invocation: false (default). Claude can decide to invoke the skill automatically when it judges the skill

data.title: THE TEACHER SKILL CATALOG.
blocks: lesson-plan-generator — Produce a draft lesson plan from learning objectives
rubric-builder — Generate a draft rubric from an assignment description
grading-workflow — Flag patterns across submissions; produce per-student flag reports
differentiation-helper — Suggest differentiation moves for a lesson plan

data.title: NEVER
blocks: · Generate content for a topic without an explicit objective input.
· Use generic activities ("students will discuss"); name the specific protocol.
· Omit the differentiation section.

data.title: COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS
blocks: "Skills are just longer prompts."
"Everything that's reusable should be a Skill."
"Skills are for power users."
"The first Skill I write will be good."
"Skills compete with CLAUDE.md."
